inference_chains.chain: orthostatic_HR_increase + dizziness + no_BP_drop -> POTS-like orthostatic intolerance branch
required_sources: active stand or tilt table HR/BP
active stand or tilt table BP

inference_chains.chain: postflight_or_bed_rest + reduced_plasma_volume + orthostatic_intolerance -> spaceflight deconditioning volume branch

required_sources: standardized HRV assessment during rest and stress tasks
inference_chains.pattern_name: Context-dependent coupling of HRV, cortisol reactivity, and stress-related neurobehavior
IBS symptoms, SCFA alterations, and mood symptoms
IBS microbiome composition/dysbiosis and mood symptoms
IBS-D permeability/LPS/endotoxemia, inflammation, and mood symptoms
IBS mast cell activation, abdominal pain, and possible mood effects
Insulin resistance associated with cognitive impairment and depression
Glucose variability associated with cognitive decline and Alzheimer's disease risk
Intermittent fasting and time-restricted eating improve metabolic health and may benefit cognitive function in older adults
Beta-hydroxybutyrate is positively associated with general cognitive performance in humans
Short-term fasting does not meaningfully impair cognitive performance in healthy adults, but longer fasts may cause modest reductions
Acute severe hypoglycemia reliably impairs cognitive performance across multiple domains
Short-term glycemic variability is associated with poorer global cognition and executive function mainly in type 2 diabetes
Typical meal-induced postprandial glucose spikes do not consistently impair cognition or fatigue in healthy populations
Subjective awareness of hypo/hyperglycemia may predict daily functioning more than sensor-detected events alone
Insufficient or disrupted sleep reduces HRV and shifts autonomic balance toward sympathetic predominance
Circadian misalignment impairs glucose regulation and increases cardiometabolic/inflammatory risk independent of total sleep time
Chronic sleep restriction produces cumulative cognitive impairment even when subjective sleepiness or fatigue plateaus
Sleep disturbance is associated with elevated inflammatory markers, especially CRP and IL-6, but effects are modest and variable
Reduced vagally mediated HRV is associated with higher fatigue, stress, anxiety/depression symptoms, and lower recovery state
Higher resting vagally mediated HRV is associated with better executive function, working memory, and attention
Acute mental stress induces sympathetic activation and vagal withdrawal
Slow breathing and HRV biofeedback can improve vagal tone, baroreflex sensitivity, mood, and stress resilience
Flattened diurnal cortisol slope or blunted CAR is associated with fatigue, burnout, and exhaustion
Acute psychological stress increases cortisol and reduces HRV/vagal tone
Chronic stress/allostatic load predicts inflammatory and metabolic dysregulation via altered HPA-axis patterns
IL-6 / systemic immune activation contributes to sickness behavior, fatigue, anhedonia, and motivation loss
Inflammation is modestly and inconsistently associated with objective cognitive impairment
Persistent post-infectious or chronic illness fatigue can occur without elevated standard peripheral inflammatory markers
Chronic centralized pain is associated with impaired attention, executive function, working memory, and processing speed
Poor sleep and affective distress partially mediate pain-related fatigue and cognitive impairment
Autonomic dysfunction and baroreflex impairment are linked to fibromyalgia symptom burden
Acute hyperventilation/hypocapnia impairs attention and cognitive inhibition via cerebral hypoperfusion
Dysfunctional breathing/hyperventilation is common in ME/CFS and long COVID with dyspnea, fatigue, and brain fog
Panic disorder is associated with chronic hypocapnia and increased CO2 sensitivity
Sleep-disordered breathing/intermittent hypoxia predicts cognitive decline risk
Slow/paced breathing improves HRV, anxiety/arousal, and attention
Dehydration/hypohydration impairs sustained attention and increases fatigue/mood disturbance
Orthostatic intolerance/POTS links volume/BP dysregulation to dizziness, brain fog, fatigue, and cognitive impairment
Regular moderate-to-vigorous physical activity improves mood, fatigue, sleep, inflammation, and cognition
Sedentary behavior increases risk of depression, fatigue, poor mood, and cognitive decline
Overexertion and post-exertional malaise can worsen symptoms in vulnerable populations
Iron deficiency with or without anemia is associated with fatigue, mood symptoms, sleep disturbance, and attention/executive deficits
Functional vitamin B12 deficiency predicts cognitive and neuropsychiatric impairment more reliably than serum B12 alone
Vitamin D, zinc, magnesium, omega-3, and folate show weaker or context-dependent links to mood, sleep, frailty, and cognition
Menstrual cycle/hormone fluctuations drive subjective mood/fatigue/sleep changes
Objective cognitive performance is largely stable across menstrual cycle in healthy adults
PMS/PMDD and mood disorders increase cycle-linked symptom vulnerability
HRV/RHR/body temperature show biphasic cycle patterns
Hormonal contraception withdrawal can worsen mood/anxiety/fatigue
Perimenopausal estradiol/progesterone variability predicts depressive vulnerability
Gut microbiota composition/function is associated with mood, fatigue, and cognitive symptoms across multiple populations
IBS/IBD symptom burden is linked to fatigue, anxiety/depression, and extraintestinal symptoms
Microbiome-targeted interventions may improve subjective GI/mood/fatigue symptoms in select groups but objective cognition effects are inconsistent
Caffeine dose/timing impairs sleep but acute caffeine may improve attention under fatigue or sleep restriction
Sedative, benzodiazepine, opioid, and anticholinergic exposure increases sedation and cognitive slowing risk, especially in older adults/polypharmacy
SSRIs/SNRIs improve mood when indicated but may cause fatigue, sleep/GI effects, HRV reduction, and withdrawal symptoms
Stimulants improve ADHD executive function but can worsen sleep, appetite, RHR/anxiety depending on dose and timing
THC/cannabis and alcohol/nicotine affect sleep, autonomic state, anxiety, and cognition in dose- and context-dependent ways
Evening blue light/light-at-night worsens sleep timing/quality and next-day cognition/alertness
Daytime/morning bright light improves alertness,mood,and selected cognitive performance
Air pollution/PM exposure is linked to cognitive decline and acute executive-function impairment
High indoor CO2/poor ventilation and moderate heat increase fatigue and reduce task performance
Noise exposure and combined environmental stressors impair sleep,mood,and higher-order cognition
Green/nature space exposure is associated with better well-being and lower distress
Migraine phases and cognitive/fatigue symptoms
Vestibular migraine and dizziness/visuospatial cognitive symptoms
Sleep disturbance and mood/autonomic comorbidity amplify migraine burden
Post-viral syndromes are associated with persistent multisystem fatigue, brain fog, dyspnea, sleep disturbance, pain, and PEM
Post-infectious dysautonomia and POTS are common in long COVID and ME/CFS-like illness
Persistent immune dysregulation and interferon/T-cell alterations are associated with long COVID symptoms
Objective cognitive deficits are present in post-viral states but subjective brain fog does not always correlate with testing or imaging
Composite allostatic load predicts adverse physical and neurobehavioral outcomes
Recovery failure requires repeated multi-signal deviation, not a single abnormal marker
Acute stress response must be distinguished from chronic allostatic overload
Orthostatic challenge can reduce cerebral blood flow and produce dizziness, fatigue, brain fog, and cognitive impairment even without systemic hypotension
Hypocapnia during upright posture reduces cerebral blood flow via vasoconstriction and can mimic POTS symptoms without tachycardia
Acute systemic immune activation induces sickness behavior
Chronic low-grade inflammation links to motivational deficits/anhedonia in depression
Kynurenine pathway bridges interferon/immune activation to depression-anhedonia risk
Impaired metabolic flexibility is associated with fatigue and exercise intolerance
ME/CFS and long COVID PEM show abnormal CPET/metabolomic recovery distinct from normal exercise fatigue
Lactate is context-dependent fuel/signaling marker, not fatigue toxin
Interoceptive constructs diverge: accuracy, sensibility and awareness are distinct
Chronic pain often combines reduced interoceptive accuracy with increased body vigilance
Prediction error models explain symptom amplification without excluding physiological abnormality
Prolonged cognitive load induces mental fatigue and selective performance decline
Subjective mental fatigue can dissociate from objective performance under compensatory motivation
Frontal theta EEG and autonomic/ocular markers track mental fatigue but require multimodal validation
Central sensitization and sensory sensitivity are associated with fatigue, mood disturbance, sleep problems, and cognitive complaints
Multisensory hypersensitivity is not limited to nociception
Self-reported central sensitivity is not equivalent to QST-confirmed nociceptive gain
Endocrine circadian rhythm disruption predicts fatigue, mood, sleep, and cognitive problems
Single-point hormone levels are less informative than rhythm loss
Shift work and environmental circadian disruption affect endocrine rhythms and neurobehavioral outcomes
NREM SO-spindle coupling supports memory consolidation
Reduced glymphatic activity associates with amyloid/tau burden and cognitive decline
Sleep fragmentation increases next-day fatigue and can impair executive inhibition
Wearable HR/RHR is reliable at rest and sleep but less reliable with motion
Wearable HRV is reliable mainly at rest/sleep
Wearable sleep duration is better supported than wearable sleep stages
No single common lab biomarker reliably infers individual fatigue/mood/cognition state
Ferritin requires inflammation context for iron inference
Functional B12 deficiency requires MMA/homocysteine context
Subjective symptoms often discordant from objective impairment
Delayed PEM and chronic symptom trajectories
Within-person deviations outperform population thresholds for state inference
DAGs and causal frameworks separate association, prediction, causation, mediation, and confounding
Contextual factors can override or downweight biomarker and wearable inference
Sparse or missing multimodal data requires explicit uncertainty and missingness modeling
Clinical and digital health models often report discrimination without adequate calibration
Graph edge promotion requires structured evidence grading and risk-of-bias assessment
Fatigue is multidimensional and requires subtype-specific inference
Subjective brain fog is weakly/inconsistently aligned with objective cognitive impairment
State-dependent brain fog can be provoked by orthostatic challenge, exertion, migraine phase, sleep deprivation, or drug timing
Dizziness word must be split into vertigo,lightheadedness,presyncope,syncope,disequilibrium,visual motion sensitivity
Orthostatic/POTS/hypocapnia dizziness requires dynamic testing; normal seated BP or SpO2 does not exclude it
Anxiety-like symptoms require differential separation from dysautonomia, respiratory, endocrine, metabolic, medication and arrhythmia mechanisms
HRV is useful but non-specific across anxiety-like states
Low recovery requires convergent multi-domain repeated deviations
Mood worsening phenotypes have distinct symptom-biomarker profiles
GI disorders form multidimensional fatigue-mood-sleep-cognition clusters
Migraine phase-dependent neurobehavioral cluster
Mitochondrial/metabolic exertional intolerance cluster
Symptom-to-phenotype-to-mechanism inference template
Multi-stage validation is required before deployment-level claims
Every graph edge requires source traceability and evidence versioning
Safety guardrail and red-team test suite is required
Acute sleep deprivation, inflammatory signaling, and attention/vigilance impairment
Acute sleep disruption, HPA-axis/cortisol dynamics, and emotional stress reactivity
Circadian misalignment, inflammatory/metabolic disruption, and cognitive performance impairment
Obstructive sleep apnea, intermittent hypoxia/inflammation, and executive-attention impairment
